jcr:content.jcr:description: DevinReactAEM Homepage - A comprehensive React + AEM demo site

hero.title: Welcome to DevinReactAEM
hero.subtitle: A full-featured React + AEM project demonstrating custom components, workflows, content fragments, and more

overview.tabContent: DevinReactAEM is a comprehensive project showcasing React integration with Adobe Experience Manager. It includes custom components with HTL/Sightly templates backed by Sling Models, React-powered interactivity, and full AEM feature coverage.

features.tabContent:
• 15+ custom AEM components with HTL and React
  • Workflow process steps (approval, tagging, notification)
  • Content Fragment models and instances
  • Experience Fragments for header/footer
  • Sling Jobs for async processing
  • OSGi schedulers for automated tasks
  • Event listeners for page, replication, and DAM events

technology.tabContent: Built with React 18, TypeScript, Java 11, OSGi, HTL/Sightly, Sling Models, and Webpack. Designed for AEM as a Cloud Service.

q1.itemTitle: How do I deploy this project?
q1.itemContent: Use Maven to build: mvn clean install -PautoInstallSinglePackage. This will deploy all modules to your local AEM Cloud SDK instance running on port 4502.

q2.itemContent: This project targets AEM as a Cloud Service SDK. Download the latest SDK from the Software Distribution portal.

q3.itemTitle: How does React integrate with AEM?
q3.itemContent: React components are built via Webpack in the ui.frontend module and output to AEM ClientLibs. HTL components include data attributes that React uses for hydration and progressive enhancement.
